revision.comment: Bot: mark 2 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23989927232|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:饗土橋姫神社]]
{{Infobox religious building |名称= 饗土橋姫神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Aedo Hashihime Shrine02.jpg |所在地 = [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]宇治今在家町 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 35.5 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 16.7 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 宇治橋鎮守神 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}所管社 |創建= [[Bunmei]]9th year （[[1477]]）以前 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= [[神嘗祭]]、宇治橋渡始式 |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067|1=Ise, Mie}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.459861|N|136.721306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity= {{ill|chinjugami|qid=Q97479976|1=Chinjugami}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Aedo Hashihime Shrine''' (饗土橋姫神社) is an auxiliary shrine (shokansha) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) within {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. It enshrines the {{ill|Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deities|lt=Guardian deity|ja|守護神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/guardian deity|qq=}} of the {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}}, located directly opposite the bridge.<ref name="ic6">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.116</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.54</ref> As the shrine's {{ill|main hall (Shinto)|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/main hall (Shinto)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/main hall (Shinto)|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3080012}} is rebuilt prior to the reconstruction of the Uji Bridge, it becomes the first {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} among the 125 shrines under the jurisdiction of Ise Grand Shrine to be rebuilt during the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengu|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinen Sengu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinen Sengu|qq=}}.<ref name="um8">Ise Bunka-sha (2009): p.58</ref> == Overview == Located about 200 [[:en:Meters|m]] from the {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}}, the entrance to the sacred precincts of the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) in {{ill|Uji-Imazaike-chō|ja|宇治今在家町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Imazaike-chō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji-Imazaike-chō|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11449448}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}<ref name="ic1">Ise Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p.111</ref>. Approximately 20 m ahead of {{ill|Aedo Hashihime Shrine|ja|饗土橋姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aedo Hashihime Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aedo Hashihime Shrine|qq=}}, you’ll find the {{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinkawa Shrine (Ise)|ja|新川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine (Ise)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinkawa Shrine (Ise)|1=Nīkawa Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11502105}}, and {{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|lt=Tsunaga Shrine|lt=Ishii Shrine (Ise)|ja|石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine (Ise)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Shrine (Ise)|qq=}}, all subordinate shrines (摂末社) of the Inner Shrine. Another 50 m further are {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|ja|大水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizu Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|ja|大水神社|lt=Kawai Shrine|lt_ja=川相神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizu Shrine|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|ja|大水神社|lt=Kumabuchi Shrine|lt_ja=熊淵神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizu Shrine|qq=}}, making a total of seven shrines clustered together<ref>Ise Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): pp.111, 116</ref>. Additionally, the predecessor to the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Library|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Library|qq=}}, the former {{ill|Hayashizaki Library|ja|林崎文庫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashizaki Library|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashizaki Library|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=神宮文庫|1=Jingū Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11589490}}, is located nearby<ref>Ise Bunka-sha, ed. (2008): p.117</ref>. Adjacent to the shrine, further along the path to the left, is the {{ill|Gōkaku Shrine|ja|合格神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōkaku Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōkaku Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135500695}}, dedicated to {{ill|Yukio Ozaki|en|Yukio Ozaki|qid=Q1152666|lt=Ozaki Yukio|1=Yukio Ozaki}}. The name "Aedo" (饗土) refers to land designated for rituals to appease or prevent the entry of evil spirits and deities associated with plagues and disasters through offerings and ceremonies<ref name="um8"/>. The shrine grounds were relocated during the construction of {{ill|Miyuki Road|ja|御幸道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyuki Road|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyuki Road|1=Miyuki Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22222736}}<ref>Ise City, ed. (2012): Vol.4, p.551</ref>. Since the reconstruction in {{ill|1969|en|1969|qid=Q2485|lt=1969|1=1969}} (Shōwa 44), the shrine has not been moved<ref name="um63">Ise Bunka-sha, ed. (2009): p.63</ref>. While the current shrine architecture follows the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} style, this was adopted during the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}. Prior to that, the shrine was built in the {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style<ref>Ise Bunka-sha, ed. (2009): p.59</ref>. {{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}} Ranks 14th among the 27 subordinate shrines of the Inner Shrine<ref>Ujiyamada City Office, ed. (1929): pp.9–10</ref>. === deity === The enshrined deity is the guardian deity of Uji Bridge (宇治橋鎮守神, Ujibashi no Mamori no Kami)<ref name="ic6"/> <ref name="gk"/>. It is venerated as the protective deity of Uji Bridge<ref name="ic6"/> <ref name="gk"/>. *{{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|lt=Niikawa Shrine|lt_ja=新川神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}} *{{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|lt=Iwai Shrine|lt_ja=石井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}} *{{ill|Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|ja|大土御祖神社|lt=Uji-no-Nuki Shrine|lt_ja=宇治乃奴鬼神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otsuchi Mioya Shrine|qq=}} == History == The origin of the {{ill|Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|lt=Uji Bridge (Ise)|ja|宇治橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uji Bridge (Ise)|qq=}} remains unknown,<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2009): p. 49</ref> and likewise, the establishment date of the {{ill|Aedo Hashihime Shrine|ja|饗土橋姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aedo Hashihime Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aedo Hashihime Shrine|qq=}}, which guards Uji Bridge, is also unclear.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.sengu.info/news/005-000150.html|title=宇治橋修造起工式おこなわれる|publisher=Ise Jingū Shikinen Sengū PR Office|date=2008-07-31|accessdate=2013-10-27|archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1027-2243-59/www.sengu.info/news/005-000150.html|archivedate=2013-10-27|qq=}}</ref> However, it is believed to have existed prior to the reconstruction of Uji Bridge in {{ill|Civilization|en|Civilization|qid=Q8432|lt=Bunmei|1=Civilization}} 9 (1477), as records indicate the shrine was rebuilt at that time.<ref name="um3">Ise Bunka-sha (2009): p. 53</ref> According to issue 211 of ''Mizugaki,'' published by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Administration Office|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Administration Office|ja_comment=former now fixed redirect|3=伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata}}, and based on "Regarding Aedo Hashihime Shrine" by Jingū priest Satoru Otowa, the large bridge resembling the current form is said to have been donated and visited by {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshinori|en|Ashikaga Yoshinori|qid=Q239170|lt=Ashikaga Yoshinori|1=Ashikaga Yoshinori}} in {{ill|Eikyō|en|Eikyō|qid=Q1193059|lt=Eikyō|1=Eikyō}} 6 (1435) as recorded in the ''Kawasaki Clan Chronicle.'' {{ill|Arakida Moriyoshi|ja|荒木田守良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida Moriyoshi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakida Moriyoshi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135501970}} explains in ''Jingū Tenryaku'' that the shrine was established in {{ill|Kanshō|en|Kanshō|qid=Q1143928|lt=Kanshō|1=Kanshō}} 7 (1467), in conjunction with Ashikaga Yoshinori's pilgrimage, modeled after the {{ill|Hashihime Shrine|lt=Hashihime Shrine|lt=Hashihime Shrine (Kyoto)|ja|橋姫神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihime Shrine (Kyoto)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihime Shrine (Kyoto)|qq=}} on the Uji Bridge in Kyoto, dedicated to {{ill|Seoritsuhime|ja|瀬織津姫|1=Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Seoritsu-hime|qq=}}. Unlike Kyoto's Uji, where shrines are placed directly on the bridge, Aedo Hashihime Shrine was never associated with tales of curses, vengeful spirits, or yokai folklore. The original location was near the famous photo spot just in front of Uji Bridge, around the torii gate and pine grove area, which previously featured two cherry trees instead of pines. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was managed by the self-governing body of Uji known as the "Uji Kaigosho."<ref name="st3">Sakurai (1991): p. 273</ref> In {{ill|Bunsei|en|Bunsei|qid=Q1327119|lt=Bunsei|1=Bunsei}} 13 (1830), the shrine burned down along with Uji Bridge.<ref name="um63"/> In June of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 5 (July 1872), the {{ill|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|en|Ministry of Religion (Japan)|qid=Q4294653|lt=Ministry of Religion|1=Ministry of Religion (Japan)}} removed shrines not listed in the ''Engishiki Jinmyōchō'' or the ''Enryaku Gishikichō'' from Jingū's jurisdiction, transferring them to the administration of {{ill|Watarai Prefecture|ja|度会県|zh|度會縣|fr|Préfecture de Watarai|1=Watarai Prefecture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486130}} and later Mie Prefecture. As a result, Aedo Hashihime Shrine was separated from Jingū’s management.<ref>Sakurai (1991): pp. 266, 272–273</ref> Uji Bridge and the torii in front of it were also transferred to Watarai Prefecture simultaneously.<ref name="um3"/> From {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 12 (1879), local residents of Uji Imazai-ke Town worshiped the shrine as their {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|qid=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}}, holding annual festivals on July 21.<ref name="st3"/> Although Uji Bridge and its torii were returned to Jingū in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 14 (1881), the shrine remained under prefectural control.<ref name="um3"/> However, in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 22 (1889), as part of the 56th {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}}, Uji Bridge was reconstructed with national funds. Since Aedo Hashihime Shrine's prefectural status posed issues for the bridge’s ceremonial usage, the shrine was reinstated under the Inner Shrine’s jurisdiction.<ref name="st3"/> Upon its return to Jingū, the shrine retained its July 21 festival to respect local traditions.<ref name="st3"/> The shrine was originally located immediately next to Uji Bridge. However, due to the construction of a national road (Miyuki Road) in December {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 40 (1907), the shrine site overlapped with the new road. Consequently, in March {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 42 (1909), a new site was established between {{ill|Ōmizu Shrine|ja|大水神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizu Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmizu Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Tsunaga Shrine|ja|津長神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunaga Shrine|qq=}}, and the shrine was relocated.<ref name="sk">Shikinaisha Research Association (1990): p. 244</ref> Since the shrine’s reconstruction in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 44 (1969), its location has remained unchanged.<ref name="um63"/> == Rites == 「宇治橋渡始式」に際して祭事が行われる<ref name="ic6"/><ref name="gk"/>。また、新しい宇治橋を建設する際に行われる「宇治橋修造起工式」にも祭儀が捧げられる<ref>{{Cite web|和書| url=http://ise.jr-central.co.jp/ujibashi/1st/report.html |title=宇治橋|参りましょう。伊勢志摩| publisher=[[東海旅客鉄道]]|date= 2008th year 7月26日 |accessdate=2013-11-02 |archiveurl=https://megalodon.jp/2013-1102-1709-53/ise.jr-central.co.jp/ujibashi/1st/report.html |archivedate=2013-11-02|qq=}}</ref>。 == Transportation Access == ;宇治橋前まで * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[参宮線]]・[[近畿日本鉄道|近鉄]][[近鉄山田線|山田線]][[伊勢市駅]]南口（JR側）または近鉄山田線・{{ill|Toba Line|en|Toba Line|qid=Q5357612|lt=鳥羽線|1=Toba Line}}{{ill|Ujiyamada Station|en|Ujiyamada Station|qid=Q7878210|lt=Ujiyamada Station|1=Ujiyamada Station}}より三重交通バスに15分ほど乗車、終点「内宮前」下車<ref name="mkr">三重県観光連盟"[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html 伊勢神宮（内宮）の観光施設・周辺情報 - 観光三重]"（2013rd year 11月2日閲覧。）</ref>。Toba Line[[五十鈴川駅]]からも三重交通バスで内宮前へ行くことが可能である。 * [[伊勢自動車道]][[伊勢西インターチェンジ|伊勢西IC]]より[[三重県道32号伊勢磯部線]]（御木本道路）を南へ約5分または[[伊勢インターチェンジ|伊勢IC]]より[[国道23号]]を南へ約5分、付近に[[駐車場]]あり<ref name="mkr"/>。 ;宇治橋前から * 宇治橋前ロータリー、内宮前駐車場を横切り、徒歩約4分（約200m）<ref name="ic1"/>。 == References == {{Reflist|2|qq=}} == Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第四巻 近代編』伊勢市、平成24th year 6月1日、1237p. * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 伊勢文化舎 編『宇治橋ものがたり』[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成21st year 10月1日、119p. ISBN 978-4-900759-39-8 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[櫻井勝之進]]『伊勢神宮の祖型と展開』[[国書刊行会]]、平成3rd year 11月30日、318p. ISBN 4-336-03296-3 * 式内社研究会 編『式内社調査報告 第六巻 東海道1』{{ill|Kogakkan University|en|Kogakkan University|qid=Q1797584|lt=Kogakkan University|1=Kogakkan University}}出版部、平成2nd year 2月28日、690p. ISBN 4-87644-080-8
== See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} * [[橋姫神社]] - [[:en:京都府|京都府]][[:en:宇治市|宇治市]]の[[宇治橋 (宇治市)|宇治橋]]を守護する神社。 * [[橋姫]] * [[瀬織津姫]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0102.html 饗土橋姫神社（あえどはしひめじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|饗土橋姫神社|version=103767156|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:饗土橋姫神社]]
<!--Aedo Hashihime Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Aedohashihime-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q17190840}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:橋の文化]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 150+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 200+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 250+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 300+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with deleted QID in ill template]]
